description: Recurring weekly tasks. Edit this file to add/remove/update recurring items.

Monday.title: Process New Expired Listings
steps: Run /expired to check for new emails from Elise and process any new expired attachments
This downloads new Expired .xlsx files, cross-references the hotsheet, filters rentals/price/cities, and creates a mailing list
New listings matching the cannonball buy box ($600k-$2M in target zips) will auto-populate into the cannonball tracker
Monday.audience: Internal -- feeds cannonball tracker + mailing list
Monday.notes: Run this BEFORE the cannonball actions so the tracker has fresh data. If no new emails from Elise, it will tell you and you can skip.

Monday.title: Expired Cannonball -- Today's Actions
steps: Read expired-cannonball-tracker.json for active campaigns
Check hotsheet CSV to verify listings are NOT back on market
Calculate which steps are due today (start_date + day offset from cannonball-cadence.json)
For new cannonballs: Go to app.listingleads.com/designs, use Expired Editor to generate personalized Sales Letter + Cover Letter
For follow-up letters: Generate using the Claude prompt from cadence file, print and mail via regular mail
For calls: Display the call script inline from Blueprints/Linked_Pages
Mark completed steps in tracker, update next_step and next_step_date
Monday.notes: This task is dynamic -- the checklist reads the tracker file and shows only what's due today. If nothing is due, it shows 'No expired actions today.'

Monday.title: Deal of the Week — Email Blast
steps: Pick hottest new listing (highest saves/views on Zillow, last 7 days)
Focus on target price point in your farm area
Copy the email template below into your email blast app
Fill in the {{variables}} with your listing's details
Send to ENTIRE database

content_files.description: Read this file and display the Subject, Preview Text, and Body directly

ready_to_use.subject: Deal of the week: {{sqft}}+ sq ft under {{price_bracket}} in {{neighborhood}}
ready_to_use.preview_text: {{save_count}} Zillow saves before you saw this.
ready_to_use.body:
{{save_count}} saves on Zillow - not a coincidence when you look at the price.

{{sqft}}+ square feet in {{neighborhood}}, under {{price_bracket}}, and the comps support why it's drawing attention.

{{bed_count}} beds, {{bath_count}} baths
{{bonus_feature}} (e.g. bonus room, attached garage, finished basement)
Under {{price_threshold}} — rare for this size in {{neighborhood}}
{{sqft}} sq ft with a {{layout_descriptor}} layout

Want the link sent over?

Monday.title: Deal of the Week — Instagram Story
steps: Open the Canva template link below
Customize with your DOTW listing details
Download as PNG, post as Instagram Story
Add Poll sticker ('Yesss!!!' / 'Yes!')
Follow up with poll responders via DM

content_files.description: Read this file for the Canva template link

Monday.title: TC Actions -- Today's Tasks
steps: Read active-transactions.json for active transactions
For each active transaction, read its Transactions/[slug]/transaction.json
Load the matching cadence file based on transaction type
Calculate which day-based steps are due (contract_date + day offset)
Show overdue steps at the TOP with OVERDUE label
For trigger-based steps, show as 'Awaiting: [trigger]' unless the event has been triggered
Display ready-to-send email templates with all variables filled in from transaction data
Mark completed steps and update next_step/next_step_date in transaction.json
Monday.notes: This task is dynamic. If no active transactions, show 'No TC actions today.'

Tuesday.title: TC Actions -- Today's Tasks
Tuesday.notes: Dynamic — see Monday definition for full steps. If no active transactions, show 'No TC actions today.'

Tuesday.title: Email of the Week (EOTW) — Value Email
steps: Copy the email template below into your email blast app
Customize for your market and personal voice
Send to ENTIRE database

content_files.description: Read this file and display Subject, Preview Text, and Body directly

ready_to_use.subject: Most homeowners forget to ask this
ready_to_use.preview_text: (I didn't want you to be one of them)
ready_to_use.body:
With everything happening in the market right now, a lot of people I talk to have no idea what their home is actually worth.

Most homeowners settle for the online estimate and move on.

But the difference between what Zillow says and what your home actually worth has been bigger than most people expect - sometimes significantly.

Not everyone thinks to ask this, but I thought you might want to know.

It's why I put together a proper equity update for my clients - actual comps, a full analysis of recent sales, and details that algorithms overlook.

It takes me some time to get it right, but there's no catch here.

Would it be helpful if I prepared one for your home?

theme_ideas: Hidden costs homeowners don't think about

Wednesday.title: TC Actions -- Today's Tasks
Wednesday.notes: Dynamic — see Monday definition for full steps. If no active transactions, show 'No TC actions today.'

Wednesday.title: Text of the Week (TOTW) — Personal Text to SOI
steps: Pick contacts from your sphere of influence (SOI)
Copy the text template below
Personalize with their first name and a local data point
Send as personal text (not mass blast)
Follow up on any responses
Wednesday.audience: SOI (Sphere of Influence) — warm contacts only

content_files.description: Read this file and display the exact text message to copy-paste

ready_to_use.text_message:
Hi [First Name], it's [Your Name].
Just ran the equity on a home about 2 minutes from yours. They gained about $14k in the last 12 months.
[Area]'s been quietly moving. Some homes more than others.
Have you checked your numbers recently?

Thursday.title: TC Actions -- Today's Tasks
Thursday.notes: Dynamic — see Monday definition for full steps. If no active transactions, show 'No TC actions today.'

Thursday.title: Instagram Content — Post, Poll, or Carousel
steps: Open the Canva template link below
Customize for this week's topic
Download and post to Instagram
For Polls: Add Poll sticker, follow up with responders
For Posts: Use the caption below

content_files.caption: Social Shareables/{{WEEK}}_Week/Thursday_*templates.txt
content_files.description: Read resources file for Canva link, templates file for caption

ready_to_use.caption:
When I studied the sales in our market, the homes that sold closest to their asking price averaged about 12 days on market.

The homes that started 10% or more above market averaged 147 days.

Same neighborhoods. Same buyers. Different pricing strategy.

And once a home sits, the psychology changes.

After about 30 days, buyers stop asking "How much is it?"

They start asking "What's wrong with it?"

Which is why pricing isn't really about the number. It's about the bracket.

Buyers don't compare your home to your neighbor's home. They compare your home to the 10-12 other homes in the same price range they're touring that weekend.

There's a number where your home gets 15 showings in a weekend. And there's a number where it gets two. That gap is smaller than most sellers think.

Thursday.title: Direct Mail (every other week)
steps: Open the Canva template link below
Edit with your info
Download as flattened PDF (Share → Download → PDF Print → Flatten)
Submit to print shop
Mail to farm area

Friday.title: TC Actions -- Today's Tasks
Friday.notes: Dynamic — see Monday definition for full steps. If no active transactions, show 'No TC actions today.'

Friday.title: Video of the Week (VOTW) — YouTube Video
steps: Download the Showflow PDF (video script) from the link below
Open the Canva thumbnail template and customize for your area
Record 5-15 minute video following the script
Upload to YouTube with the title, description, and hashtags below
Cross-post to Instagram Reels and TikTok

content_files.description: Read resources for Showflow PDF + Canva thumbnail link, templates for title/description/hashtags

ready_to_use.video_title: If I Were Moving To [AREA] in 2026, I'd Move Here

In this video, I'm sharing [NUMBER] under-the-radar places I'd seriously consider if I were making the move myself — with real price ranges, honest lifestyle breakdowns, and exactly who each community is right for.
